goal: Implement the first v0.3.11 lean protocol core: lean paths, state schema, schema validation, gate validation, role binding schema, bypass approval schema, review status/decision validation, decision_needed blocking logic, and cross-entry gate decision consistency tests.

approval_evidence.summary: Human repeatedly approved continuing after the v0.3.11 plan and second third-party review, including the latest '同意'.

reason_for_transition_contract: Legacy v0.3.10 preflight requires an active .governance/changes package, but v0.3.11 work is explicitly intended to remove that heavy default model. Recreating legacy heavy directories for this implementation would conflict with the approved reset goal.
scope_out: Deleting or reverting existing user changes in .governance/AGENTS.md
Deleting or reverting existing user changes in .governance/agent-playbook.md
Creating .governance/changes as the default v0.3.11 model
Creating .governance/archive, .governance/runtime, .governance/index, or .governance/local as normal v0.3.11 runtime output
Tagging, pushing, or publishing v0.3.11 without explicit final release authorization
acceptance: Tests are written before production implementation for this first batch.
Lean default read set is fixed and small.
State schema contains compact plan, verification plan, concrete role bindings, bypass approval evidence, review matrix, and decision_needed lifecycle.

Gate validation blocks missing approval evidence, missing participant initialization, self-review without bypass evidence, failed verify, rejected review, open blocking decisions, and unapproved closeout.
The same state input yields the same gate decision through the shared helper used by different entrypoints.
No legacy heavy governance directory is created by first-batch tests.
expected_outputs: Passing targeted v0.3.11 tests for lean state and gates.
A concise implementation summary with remaining risks.
